title: NIS2 Business Continuity and Crisis Management Monitoring

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(c) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Shows BCM plans, BIA reviews, and failover tests stay on schedule by matching ServiceNow GRC policies plus `category=bcp_review` tasks to `nis2_bcp_inventory`, and surfaces ITSI services that leave 'green' during `is_drill` exercises.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.2.4` on `index=itsm` (`sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy`, `sourcetype=snow:task`) and `index=itsi` `sourcetype=itsi:service_health`, enriched via `lookup nis2_bcp_inventory` (plan_id, owner, review_cycle_days, last_review_date, last_test_date); exports/PDF to vault with RFC3161. SME review required.

dataSources: `index=itsm` with Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) normalized pulls: `sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` (plan identifiers, `sys_updated_on`, workflow state, policy classification) and `sourcetype=snow:task` for `category=bcp_review` work (task number, assignment group, closed_at, test_date). `index=itsi` with Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841): `sourcetype=itsi:service_health` (service_id, service_name, service_health_status, drill_window flags if tagged). CSV lookup `nis2_bcp_inventory.csv` joins plan_id to review_cycle_days, last_review_date, last_test_date, owner, and BIA refresh markers.
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841); define `nis2_bcp_inventory` in `transforms.conf` on the Search Head
spl:
(index=itsm (sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy OR (sourcetype=snow:task category=bcp_review)) earliest=-180d@d) OR (index=itsi sourcetype=itsi:service_health is_drill=1 earliest=-24h)
| eval plan_key=if(index="itsm",coalesce(plan_id,u_bcp_plan_ref,cmdb_ci),service_name)
| lookup nis2_bcp_inventory plan_id AS plan_key OUTPUT plan_name, owner, review_cycle_days, last_review_date, last_test_date
| eval last_review_epoch=if(isnotnull(last_review_date),strptime(last_review_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),null())
| eval days_since_review=if(index="itsm" AND isnotnull(last_review_epoch),round((now()-last_review_epoch)/86400,1),null())
| eval review_breach=0
| eval review_breach=if(index="itsm" AND ((days_since_review>review_cycle_days) OR isnull(last_test_date) OR match(lower(state),"pending|waiting|work in progress")),1,review_breach)
| eval review_breach=if(index="itsi" AND NOT match(service_health_status,"(?i)normal|green"),1,review_breach)
| where review_breach=1
| stats latest(service_health_status) as svc_status latest(days_since_review) as days_since_review latest(owner) as plan_owner values(plan_name) as plans by plan_key index
| sort 0 - days_since_review
description: Correlates ServiceNow GRC and BCM task evidence with a BCP inventory lookup and ITSI service-health rows during declared drills, surfacing overdue plan reviews, missing failover test dates, stalled workflow states, and services that breach health expectations while `is_drill=1`.
value: Instead of chasing spreadsheets before an audit, executives get a dated list of which continuity artefacts are out of cadence and which practice runs actually stressed the production service tree — so gaps are fixed before regulators ask and crisis calls start with facts, not guesses.
implementation: (1) Install Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) and enable the GRC/policy and task pulls your process already uses; map `category=bcp_review` for BCM work. (2) Load `nis2_bcp_inventory.csv` via Lookup Editor or `lookups/` + `transforms.conf`. (3) Tag ITSI entities used in failover drills; ensure `itsi:service_health` includes `is_drill` during exercises. (4) Schedule the saved search weekly and push results to the compliance dashboard. (5) Archive outputs with RFC3161 timestamping per `evidenceSigning`.
visualization: Dashboard Studio row A: table of `plan_key`, `plan_owner`, `days_since_review`, BIA freshness flag from lookup. Row B: ITSI Service Analyzer deep link for drill services in breach. Row C: single value of open `snow:task` counts stuck >14 days.
knownFalsePositives:
**ServiceNow backlog grooming:** Distinguish long-lived `snow:task` items already waived by risk acceptance in the ticket. Suppress with `lookup nis2_bcp_exception_register exception_key AS number OUTPUT suppress_until | where now() > suppress_until OR isnull(suppress_until)`.

**Vendor field renames after upgrade:** Distinguish missing `plan_id` because the field moved to a new alias. Suppress nothing — fix `props.conf` FIELDALIAS in Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) until `plan_key` populates again.

**Planned maintenance on ITSI KPIs during drills:** Distinguish `service_health_status` red caused by known change windows from real failover failure. Suppress with `lookup maintenance_windows_drills service_name OUTPUT in_window | where in_window!=1` or a time-bound exception row.

**Historical imports inflating “days since review”:** Distinguish bulk-loaded policies with synthetic `last_review_date`. Suppress via filter `where source!="legacy_migrate"` after tagging those sources during onboarding.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search tier with role `nis2_bcp_reader` granting `index=itsm`, `index=itsi`, and `srchLookups` for `nis2_bcp_inventory`.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) ≥8.x on a Heavy Forwarder or data collection node with OAuth / basic credential stored in the credential store; enable modular inputs that hydrate `sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` and `sourcetype=snow:task` at ≤15 minute intervals for BCM categories.
• Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841) with Service Analyzer access; drill tagging convention `is_drill=1` on `sourcetype=itsi:service_health` rows for the regulated entity tree (configure via ITSI episode policy or alert aggregation during exercises).
• CSV lookup `lookups/nis2_bcp_inventory.csv` deployed through the shared compliance app with `transforms.conf` stanza `[nis2_bcp_inventory]` and `check_matches = warn` during QA.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
In `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_snow/local/inputs.conf`, duplicate the vendor template stanzas for GRC policy tables and `sc_task` / `task` pulls, setting `index = itsm`, `polling_interval = 300`, and `start_by_shell = false` as recommended in the TA docs. Map table columns to `plan_id`, `state`, `category`, and `cmdb_ci` via `props.conf` FIELDALIAS so the SPL `plan_key` coalesce resolves consistently. For ITSI, validate KV store collections `itsi_group_entities` include CMDB keys that line up with `plan_key`. Run `splunk cmd splunkd print-modinput-config --serveruri https://localhost:8089 Splunk_TA_snow://<input>` from the CLI to confirm the modular input returns HTTP 200 and JSON payloads under 5 MB per poll.

Step 2 — Create the search, test panel, and alert
Use Search & Reporting → New Search, paste the UC SPL, set time range Last 180 days, then Save As → Alert with cron `0 7 * * MON` and throttle 24 hours on `plan_key`. Add a drilldown that opens `ServiceNow -> GRC -> Policies` filtered by `plan_key` and a second drill that opens ITSI **Service Analyzer** for `service_name`. Understanding this SPL: the outer search isolates BCM artefacts; `lookup nis2_bcp_inventory` supplies contractual review cadence; `eval review_breach` turns policy data into a boolean; the `append` subsearch pulls only drill-window health rows so failover evidence is not drowned by steady-state noise.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick three plans from the CSV and manually confirm `last_review_date` in ServiceNow matches the Splunk row within one business day. Run `| makeresults | eval plan_key="TEST-PLAN-01" | lookup nis2_bcp_inventory plan_id AS plan_key OUTPUT *` to verify the lookup path. In ITSI, execute a coordinated drill, watch `index=itsi sourcetype=itsi:service_health is_drill=1 earliest=-4h` in Search, and confirm severity lines up with the Service Analyzer tile for the same `service_name`.

Step 4 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot
Publish a Dashboard Studio board with tokens for `plan_owner` and `service_name`. Wire an adaptive response (email or webhook) that embeds the RFC3161 token reference produced by your evidence pipeline. Weekly governance: export the panel to PDF, store in the records tool, and attach the timestamp manifest.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No `snow:sn_grc_policy` events** — input disabled or wrong endpoint: check **Settings → Data inputs → Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow**, validate REST path `/api/now/table/sn_grc_policy`, rotate API credentials if HTTP 401 appears in `splunkd.log`.
• **Lookup misses every row** — casing mismatch on `plan_id`; normalize with `| eval plan_key=lower(plan_key)` before lookup or rebuild CSV keys.
• **`append` returns nothing** — ITSI macro not setting `is_drill=1`; temporarily remove `is_drill=1` filter, confirm `sourcetype=itsi:service_health` exists, then re-enable tagging via episode workflow.
• **False overdue reviews** — CSV `review_cycle_days` stale; refresh the file and bump `last_updated` column tracked in a helper search.
• **RBAC errors** — user missing `itsm` or `itsi` index: add indexes to the role via **Settings → Access controls → Roles**.
grandmaExplanation: We check that your emergency plans are reviewed on time, that practice runs are actually recorded, and that when you rehearse a failover the important services still look healthy. If something slips, you see it early instead of during an auditor visit.
exclusions: Does not certify legal conformity of the directive itself, file formal supervisory notifications, sign off board minutes, or replace qualified legal review of crisis communications.
evidence: Weekly saved search `UC-22.2.4`, Dashboard Studio panel export, RFC3161-qualified PDF or CSV bundle stored in the restricted evidence index with owner `Head of IT Operations`.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Insert a BCM policy row in ServiceNow whose `last_review_date` in `nis2_bcp_inventory` is 400 days old while `review_cycle_days` is 365, and confirm the alert fires naming `plan_key`, `plan_owner`, and the open `snow:task` number; during an ITSI drill set `is_drill=1` on a non-green service and verify the appended subsearch lists `service_name` with `unhealthy_drill_signals>0`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Update the inventory so `last_review_date` is within cycle, close related tasks to `Complete`, keep ITSI services green throughout the drill window, and confirm the scheduled search returns zero breach rows after the 7 a.m. Monday run while the audit dashboard still shows the last successful empty snapshot timestamp.
